user_message: Working on Iron Creek Mining's escalation triage -- need you to prep a brief.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-22 16:06 EST
Subject: escalation triage - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-11 17:58 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining escalation triage

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 17:25 EST
Subject: escalation triage - Action Required

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-03-27
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Kevin (Iron Creek Mining)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:14:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:21:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:29:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:32:00] Kevin (Iron Creek Mining): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
